plant.title: Monstera
plant.summary: Contains insoluble calcium oxalate crystals that cause intense burning and irritation of the mouth, tongue, and lips in cats. While rarely fatal, it causes significant discomfort.
plant.what_to_do:
**Mild to moderate emergency—seek veterinary care.**

1. Rinse your cat’s mouth gently with water (do not force).
2. Offer a small amount of milk or tuna water to soothe irritation.
3. Call your vet for advice—most cases resolve with supportive care.
4. Watch for breathing difficulty (rare but serious).

> 💡 Pro tip: Keep Monsteras on high shelves—they’re climbers and love to drape!
sources: ASPCA Toxic and Non-Toxic Plants Database
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine
plant.contentHtml: ⚠️ Disclaimer: PawSafePlants provides general educational information only. This content is not veterinary advice. If your cat has been exposed to any plant, contact a licensed veterinarian or the ASPCA Animal Poison Control Center immediately. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of this information. Use at your own risk.
